title: cassia cushina
intro: Cassia couchiana, also known as Cassia cuneata or Cassia cuneifolia, is a plant species in the legume family. It is a shrub native to tropical and subtropical regions, valued for its medicinal properties. The plant's roots and leaves contain anthraquinones, which contribute to its medicinal benefits. Cassia couchiana has been traditionally used for its laxative properties, and in herbal preparations, it is often combined with other herbs to promote digestion and relieve constipation.
